description: Multi-profile support: multiple isolated profiles (each with own DB, settings, attachments). Profile selector dialog at startup or from Settings. Create new / open existing / switch profiles. Each profile is a separate directory. Last used profile remembered. Profile manager accessible from Settings view.
steps: Create app/services/profile_manager.py: ProfileManager class with methods: list_profiles() -> list[ProfileInfo] (scan parent directory for profile directories), create_profile(name: str) -> ProfileInfo (create new directory with empty DB), switch_profile(profile_path: Path) -> None (update last_used in global config), get_current_profile() -> ProfileInfo, get_last_used_profile() -> Path|None. ProfileInfo dataclass: name, path, db_size, last_modified. Store global profile registry in a JSON file at the app data parent level (e.g. %APPDATA%/Darts/profiles.json).

Update app/runtime_paths.py: add get_profiles_registry_path() returning the parent-level profiles.json path. Modify _resolve_profile_root() to check profiles.json for last-used profile path (if not overridden by env var).

Create app/ui/profile_selector_dialog.py: QDialog shown at startup (or on demand). Shows list of profiles with name, last modified, size. Buttons: Открыть, Создать новый, Удалить. On selection, updates last-used and triggers app restart hint or direct switch.
Update app/ui/settings_view.py: add 'Управление профилями' section with button to open ProfileSelectorDialog. Show current profile name and path.

Update app/__main__.py: before MainWindow creation, check if multiple profiles exist or if this is first launch. If multiple profiles and no last-used, show ProfileSelectorDialog. Otherwise use last-used profile.

Add tests in tests/test_profile_manager.py: test create_profile creates directory with valid structure, test list_profiles finds existing profiles, test switch_profile updates registry, test isolation (two profiles have independent DBs).
acceptance_criteria: Multiple profiles can be created with unique names
Each profile has isolated DB, settings, and directory structure
Switching profiles updates the global registry
Profile selector dialog shows all available profiles
Last-used profile is remembered and loaded on next launch
Current profile name shown in status bar and settings
findings: All 10 new profile manager tests pass. Full suite: 205 passed, 70 skipped. mypy clean. Created ProfileManager service with create/list/delete/switch profiles, registry-based last_used tracking. Updated runtime_paths.py with get_profiles_base_dir(), get_profiles_registry_path(), and modified _resolve_profile_root() to check registry for last_used. Created ProfileSelectorDialog with open/create/delete buttons. Added profile section to SettingsView showing current profile and management button.
